name: Hello
description: Minimal endpoint that responds with a greeting. The interesting bit isn't the workflow — it's that the operator has called WorkflowRegistry.getInstance().useGlobalMiddleware(["request-id", "audit-log"]) at process boot, so EVERY workflow in the process (this one included) runs those two middleware first, without any per-trigger or per-workflow config. The workflow itself stays minimal. Demonstrates: process-wide middleware that wraps every workflow without touching workflow files. Useful for ops concerns (request IDs, audit logging, OpenTelemetry spans) that should apply uniformly. Resolution order: process-global -> trigger-level -> workflow-level -> workflow steps.
